rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CAROLYN S. BROWN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/24/2020 (no earlier date available, pursuant to court directive). The following issues must be addressed: 1) Paragraph 3 of the report only alleges that there is no heir/beneficiary being held in prison, jail, or other local correctional facility, but notice under Probate Code section 9202(b) is also required if any heir/beneficiary has previously been confined in any correctional facility including any road camp and industrial farm. A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner regarding this issue must be filed. 2) A partial inventory and appraisal listing all newly discovered assets described in the report must be filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOE W. THORN SR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/24/20 per court directive. Furthermore, the "supplement" filed on 1/21/20 did not fully address all the issues with the accounting petition. Probate Code Section 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. If the executor is unable to initiate a formal estate proceeding in Oregon due to health issues, she should consider resigning as the personal representative or seek to have someone in Oregon seek appointment as the personal representative there. The cost of appraisal for a piece of property that is not an asset of this estate (212 Amber Ct.) should not be borne by this estate. The copy of the SW3 partnership agreement does not show how the capital contribution amounts paid out of this estate were determined. No Corrected (Final) Inventory and Appraisal has been filed yet.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ARLENE H. SULLIVAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/24/20 per court directive. Additionally, the "Petitioner's Response to Examiner's Notes," which should have been an AMENDMENT to the petition, not referring to Notes that are not filed for the record, amended (increased) the costs to be reimbursed to the attorney. An amendment requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] The list of attorney costs, furthermore, still shows lump sums, such as the amount of $541.85 on 11/2/18 for "Filing Fee," etc., when the filing fee was $450.00, and the amount of $505.50 on 7/24/19, when the filing fee was $435.00. Notice was mailed to Jameson River Haymes in care of Ethan Haymes, when it has now been disclosed that he lives with Stephanie Ferrell, who has waived notice but whose address has not been set forth; direct notice to the minor has not been given as required by CRC 7.51(d).

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE VIOLET T. DUNNINGS REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Angelique Mcguire'S Petition For Instructions And For Removal Of Trustees, Appointment Of A Receiver Or Temporary Trustee, An Accounting, To Surcharge
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/24/20 per court directive. Additionally, notice is defective: the Notice of Hearing does not set forth the full caption of the pleading and notice mailed on 2/4/20 was not at least 30 days before the 2/19/20 hearing. The proof of service filed on 3/9/20 (no courtesy copy received yet as of 3/23/20) was not properly verified (not dated) and also shows that notice was both deposited with the USPS and placed in an envelope for collection and mailing following the firm's ordinary business practices. That notice of hearing also had the incomplete caption of the petition and was purportedly mailed on 3/9/20, which was not at least 30 days before 4/1/20.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID ROBERT SENECHEK
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/24/20 per court directive. Additionally, a certified copy of Letters appointing the petitioner as the personal representative of Decedent's mother's estate in Florida must be filed. (An unendorsed filed copy is not sufficient.) The petitioner searched only the San Francisco Domestic Partnership Registration, not the state registration, and only for the period beginning 2/23/10, whereas, her searches for public or confidential marriages in San Francisco extended back to 1975 and 1972, respectively. Petitioner has not sufficiently addressed who the SRDP informant is, who is named on Decedent's death certificate, and if she has tried to contact him.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS IVELI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/24/20 per Court directive. Also, no courtesy copies of any papers filed after 2/13/20 have been received by the assigned Examiner as of 3/23/20. In contested matters, Examiners are prohibited from any ex parte communication with one party; any call or email that does not include all parties will not be answered. Courtesy copies of all papers filed after 2/13/20 must be delivered to the Examiner by no later than 6/17/20 (or one week before the hearing date, should matters have to be continued further).

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE NANCY J. WHITE LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Compel Distribution Of Trust Assets And Termination Of Trust; Or In The Alternative, To Compel Account And To Appoint Independent Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/30/2020 due to court directive. Issues: Examiner does not have the required courtesy copy of the petition or proposed order. File stamped single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 2.7, 14.6). A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL SHIU KIN NG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/22/2020 (no earlier date available, pursuant to court directive) to be heard with the competing petition filed by Siu Foon Ng. The following issues must be resolved: 1) No Declaration of Real Property has been filed by the contestant petitioner. See LRSF 14.27C. 2) No courtesy copies of the documents filed by the contestant petitioner have been provided. 3) No proposed orders from the contestant petitioner have been provided. 4) No proof of publication has been filed by the contestant petitioner. 5) No declination signed by the decedent's surviving spouse who has priority for appointment over the contestant petitioner has been filed. See LRSF 14.33. 6) Item 3d(3) of the petition refers to "50%" of personal property. Does this reflect the decedent's 50% tenancy in common interest as his separate property or his 50% share of community property? 7) Item 6g(1)(c) was checked, despite the will contest. 8) Notice of the hearing on the competing petition must be served on the alternate executor named in the contested will. See Probate Code section 8110(b). A supplemental proof of service must be filed. 9) A summons has not been served on all the parties entitled to notice under Probate Code section 8110. See Probate Code section 8250(a).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT GELHAUS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/24/20 per court directive. Additionally, no courtesy copies and no proposed order were received by the assigned Examiner as of 3/23/20. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.6 A., and 14.93 G.] Item 8 of the petition must identify the decedent's parents and set forth their dates of death as well as the identities and dates of death of the ancestors of the proposed personal representative, who is reportedly a cousin, to confirm his relationship. [LRSF 14.26 D. and E.] A non-resident personal representative is required to post bond even if all heirs waive bond. A declaration must be filed, setting forth the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate. [LRSF 14.37.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF IAN JAMES BATEEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO DETERMINE SUCCESSION TO REAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/24/2020 (no earlier date available, pursuant to court directive). The following issues must be addressed: 1) Notice of the hearing and proof of service must be filed. 2) No proposed orders have been provided. 3) The decedent's real property must be listed in the inventory and appraisal and must be appraised by a probate referee. 4) A supplement, signed and verified by the petitioner, addressing the following issues, must be filed: A) The petitioner is not a successor of the decedent. What is the petitioner's relationship to the decedent and Robert/Andrea Bateen listed as the successors? B) Item 9a(3) or (4) of the petition must be checked. C) Attachment 11 is missing. D) The box "personal property" must be checked in item 13 of the petition.
